gaps.title: Schema path resolution after pi-jit-agents extraction — how do agent YAMLs reference schemas when 15 workflow output schemas stay in pi-workflows and agent YAMLs stay in their origin packages
gaps.description: Agent YAMLs reference output schemas + contextBlocks schemas via relative paths that must resolve correctly from each package's directory layout. With consumer migration per FEAT-001/TASK-082/TASK-083 in flight + the canonical body realization per DEC-0004 dual-mode dispatch landing, the schema resolution path needs canonical specification. Options surfaced in v1 Q2: (a) resolveSchemaPath() accepts the agent's load directory as base — fully tier-aware; (b) Agent YAMLs use prefixed references (block:name, workflow-schema:name) resolved by jit-agents against known registries; (c) Agent YAMLs reference schemas by absolute path within the package (e.g. @davidorex/pi-workflows/schemas/verifier-output.schema.json).

evidence.reference: Open question carried forward from v1 §7 'Open Work Before This Extraction Can Be Planned'. v2 inherits Q2; resolution is operator scope-decision among the three surfaced options.

gaps.impact: Schema reference resolution determines whether agent YAMLs are package-portable (option c) or registry-routed (option b) or load-directory-rooted (option a). Affects how 15 workflow output schemas relocate during FEAT-001 consumer cascade + how downstream operators author new agent specs.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: schema path resolution; tier-aware schema lookup
gaps.proposed_resolution: Operator scope-decision among options a/b/c. Recommend: option (b) prefixed references (block:name + workflow-schema:name + agent-schema:name namespaces) — most consistent with the pi-context block: sentinel pattern already used for contextBlocks; package-portability via registry resolution; no per-package absolute paths needed.
